evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 frames classifier as a pre-screening triage that trims references prior to human abstract screening, and humans still define class membership and exclude off-scope (non-English, non-neuroscience) items.
notes: Upgraded low to high: Abstract and Introduction directly frame triage-for-screening; Methods Section 2.2.2 shows humans set scope and exclusions.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 is a classification benchmarking study; it makes no causal or mechanism claims and does not constrain mechanism interpretation.
notes: Upgraded low to high not_applicable: full Methods confirms task is label prediction, not causal inference.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 shows humans defining 15 (later 14) mutually exclusive study-type classes, iterating guidelines over 3 rounds, and making post-hoc boundary decisions (dropping Animal-systematic-review; redefining intervention).
notes: Upgraded low to high and changed observable no to yes, status not_applicable to robust: Methods Section 2.2.2 documents construct-definition work in detail.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 draws only from PubMed neuroscience (CNS/mental-disorder MeSH) with a convenience sample of ~2,500 abstracts; authors acknowledge coverage limits of MeSH but do not broaden corpora, so pluralism is weakly engaged.
notes: Upgraded medium to high and refined observable yes to partial, status conditional to unclear: Methods Section 2.2.1 shows single-database single-field sampling; pluralist role is largely absent rather than robust.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 documents iterative annotation guidelines, stratified train/validation/test splits, reproducibility-badged open data, and an OSF protocol link (osf.io/3yxqh), indicating heavy human protocol/provenance work.
notes: Upgraded medium to high and status conditional to robust: Methods Section 2.2 plus open-materials/open-data badges directly evidence architect role.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 does not extrapolate across populations or settings; performance is reported on an in-domain neuroscience test split with no transportability claim.
notes: Upgraded low to high not_applicable: full Methods confirms narrow evaluation scope.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 involves no interviews, fieldwork, or situated interpretation; all annotation is desk-based metadata labeling.
notes: Upgraded low to high not_applicable: Methods describe only text-based annotation from PubMed metadata.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 records shared first authorship and author contributions but does not discuss AI disclosure, responsibility for classifier errors, or reallocation of review-team accountability.
notes: Upgraded low to medium and observable no to partial: authorship is handled conventionally but AI-specific accountability is not engaged.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 shows a clear split-candidate signature: humans devote substantial effort to category boundaries (14 mutually exclusive classes, hierarchy, revised guidelines) that is analytically distinct from other construct work.
notes: Upgraded low to high and observable no to yes, status not_applicable to robust: Methods Section 2.2.2 directly exhibits this split role.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 resolves all inter-rater conflicts by discussion to a single label and does not preserve subgroup disagreement; only Cohen's Kappa is reported, not a pluralistic label schema.
notes: Upgraded low to high not_applicable: Methods Section 2.2.2 explicitly collapses disagreement.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 does not test cross-population or cross-design transportability; evaluation stays within the neuroscience PubMed corpus.
notes: Upgraded low to high not_applicable: Methods restrict evaluation to in-domain split.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 is fundamentally a benchmarking study: humans build a human-annotated reference corpus and calibrate GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and seven BERT variants against it using F1/precision/recall with CIs.
notes: Upgraded low to high and observable no to yes, status not_applicable to robust: Tables 1-3 and Methods Section 2.3-2.4 directly exhibit this role.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 has no interviewer or fieldwork component; annotation is desk-based on PubMed text.
C008_P1 relies on annotator domain knowledge (neuroscience methods, trial design) to apply the codebook, but this is tacit rather than framed as an explicit context-witness role.
notes: Upgraded low to medium and observable no to partial: guideline application implies background expertise, though the paper does not foreground it.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 explicitly positions automated classification as a way to reduce the labor of title/abstract triage, with BERT F1 >0.8 suggesting meaningful pre-screening delegation to AI.
notes: Upgraded low to high: Abstract, Highlights, and Discussion-adjacent framing directly support shrinking.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 shows humans shifting from being the sole coders to serving as ground-truth providers and auditors against which fine-tuned BERT models (F1 ~0.84) are benchmarked.
notes: Upgraded low to high and observable no to yes, status not_applicable to shrinking: Table 3 shows BERT approaching human-annotation quality on anchored labels.

evidence_basis_summary: C008_P1 makes no claim that LLMs drafted the paper or polished prose; the AI role is confined to study-type classification.
notes: Upgraded low to high not_applicable: paper scope is classifier benchmarking, not authorship assistance.
